name: Enroll an existing loyalty program member - success
description: Successful club member enrollment. Request enrolls an existing loyalty program member via loyaltyMembershipNumber; response returns the resulting club membership number, null message, and status true (Club Member Enrollment Details schema).
notes: Resource definition at lines 20570-20722; HTTP method POST only; resource Available version 64.0 (URI params also 64.0). The resource path is documented WITHOUT a /services/data/vXX.X prefix (bare: loyalty-programs/{programName}/clubs/{clubName}/member-enrollment); I prepended /services/data/v64.0 to match Salesforce REST convention seen elsewhere in the guide, strip the prefix if the mock expects the bare path. URI params programName and clubName are oddly typed as Integer in the doc table though descriptions say they are names (likely a doc typo; treated as path string segments). Request body has FIVE documented variants (existing member, existing contact, new person, existing account, new account), I built fixtures for variant 1 (existing member) and variant 5 (new account). Property table doc casing is split across line wraps; canonical field names inferred from JSON examples: enrollmentDate, clubMembershipNumber, loyaltyMembershipNumber, contactId, accountId, associatedContactDetails, associatedAccountDetails, memberStatus, membershipEndDate, membershipBy. associatedAccountDetails references "Loyalty Account Input" (page 499) and associatedContactDetails references "Loyalty Contact Input" (page 499), nested schemas not expanded here beyond the documented JSON example fields. Response schema = "Club Member Enrollment Details" (lines 28779-28799): only fields are clubMembershipNumber (String), message (String, explains failure), status (Boolean). Response-body property table lists Available Version 63.0 for the fields while the resource itself is 64.0. The documented JSON response example (line 28782) is the success case (message null, status true). The doc does NOT show an explicit error/failure JSON example, message+status:false would represent failure per field descriptions, but I did not fabricate one since no example is shown; both fixtures are documented success-shaped. No HTTP error-status example documented.
